stories: Good morning Sean, it's Thursday, the sixteenth of April, 2026
On this day in 1972, NASA launched Apollo 16 — John Young, Ken Mattingly, and Charles Duke headed for the Moon's Descartes Highlands
Young and Duke spent three days on the surface, collecting nearly 100 kilograms of lunar samples and driving the lunar rover further from the lander than any crew before them
Young later commanded the very first Space Shuttle mission
A fine reminder of what a well-planned Thursday can achieve
Over in Singapore, two stories this morning
The case is renewing international attention on Singapore's mandatory drug sentencing laws
In lighter news: Singapore's new two-power-bank-per-passenger aircraft rule came fully into effect yesterday, with 15 devices confiscated at checkpoints on the very first day

Good morning Sean, it's Wednesday, the fifteenth of April, 2026
Today is the birthday of Leonardo da Vinci - born in Tuscany on this day in 1452
He never considered any field off-limits, and that is worth carrying into the day
Over in Singapore, the Monetary Authority has tightened its monetary policy settings in response to yesterday's Q1 growth figures
6 percent year on year in the first quarter - solid on the surface - but the central bank flagged that growth will slow through the rest of the year
MAS raised its 2026 inflation forecast to between 1
The driver is energy price risk from the US-Iran conflict, with oil holding above 100 dollars a barrel
Worth noting for travellers - Singapore Airlines is continuing to reroute its Asia-Europe services around closed Iranian airspace
